pages_preview.text_preview: QUESTIONNAIRE I.D. INTERNATIONAL FOOD POLICY RESEARCH INSTITUTE (IFPRI) IMPACT EVALUATION OF COMMUNITY ADVOCACY FORUMS (BARAZAS) IN UGANDA SUBCOUNTY QUESTIONNAIRE INTRODUCTION TO BE GIVEN TO THE RESPONDENT: Hello. My name is ______. I am working for International Food Policy Research Institute (IFPRI), an organization that conducts research on development challenges related to accessibility to agricultural goods and services, markets, health services, education services and other infrastructure in Uganda and elsewhere. We are here to request your participation in a study that we are conducting with the Office of the Prime Minister (OPM) throughout Uganda. This is part of a research program which will run up to 2017. Our objective of is to understand the existing status of service provision in health, education and NAADS (agriculture) to rural households, and whether they can be improved through the Barazas. As part of this survey we are interviewing households, government officials and service providers across four regions of the country. Through this, we hope to first understand the constraints you face in doing your work, and second, to understand whether Barazas would have an effect on government services. I would like to inform you that your participation in this study is voluntary. You may choose to discontinue this interview at any time or skip any questions that you think make you uncomfortable but there is no effect or penalty to that. While you do not gain any direct benefit from participation, this will benefit you in the long run as it will influence the government policy on how best to serve citizens. I would also like to assure you that the information you provide to us will be kept confidential at all times and that your answers will be used only as aggregate with other responses collected across the country to gauge public service delivery in Uganda. I am therefore requesting for about 20 minutes of your time to talk to you. Can I go ahead 1 Yes 2 No In case you need more information, you can contact: Dr. N.
